We employed three modelling approaches that have successfully been applied in previous studies on species distribution (Guisan and Zimmermann 2000): generalised linear models (GLM, i.e. logistic regression in this case), generalised additive models (GAM), and classification and regression trees‚Ä¶

…nest burrows in close proximity of one another appears to be well founded as previously shown by several studies that measured distances between kin vs. non-kin nest burrows, including in long-term data sets (King 1989b; Viblanc et al. 2010; Arnaud, Dobson & Murie 2012; Dobson et al. 2012).
This result is consistent with the conclusions of the aforementioned recent study of (34) and reinforces them from a significantly broader perspective.
